content.problemStatement:
VK gave a problem to Chef, but Chef is too lazy, so he asked you to solve the problem for him. The statement of the problem follows.

Consider an integer with NNN digits (in decimal notation, without leading zeroes) D1,D2,D3,…,DND_1, D_2, D_3, \dots, D_ND1​,D2​,D3​,…,DN​. Here, D1D_1D1​ is the most significant digit and DND_NDN​ the least significant. The weight of this integer is defined as ∑i=2N(Di−Di−1) .\sum_{i=2}^N (D_i - D_{i-1})\,.∑i=2N​(Di​−Di−1​).

You are given integers NNN and WWW. Find the number of positive integers with NNN digits (without leading zeroes) and weight equal to WWW. Compute this number modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and W denoting the number of digits and the required weight.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of N-digit positive integers with weight W, modulo 10^9 + 7.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The two-digit integers with weight 3 are 14, 25, 36, 47, 58, 69.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming to calculate the number of N-digit positive integers with weight W. We can define a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the number of i-digit positive integers with weight j. We can then fill up this array using the base cases and the recurrence relation. Finally, we can return dp[N][W] as the answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * |W|) — explain: per query is O(N * |W|), total is O(T * N * |W|)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * |W|) — explain: we need to store the dp array of size N * |W|
